description_long: This object is a rectangular segment of a stone wall, constructed from irregularly shaped, medium-sized stones tightly packed together with visible mortar in the gaps. The stones vary in color from light to dark gray, with occasional hints of brown and tan, giving the surface a natural, rugged appearance. The wall's face is uneven, with protruding and recessed stones creating a highly textured, rough surface. The top and bottom edges are straight, while the sides are slightly tapered, suggesting it may be part of a retaining or boundary wall. The side views reveal the wall's thin profile, indicating it is a single layer of stone rather than a thick structure. This type of stonework is commonly found in urban parks, historical districts, or as decorative barriers along sidewalks and plazas.
description: A rugged, rectangular stone wall segment made of tightly packed, irregular gray stones with a rough, textured surface and visible mortar. The wall is thin and slightly tapered, typical of urban decorative or boundary barriers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the stone wall, highlighting the irregular stone arrangement and rough texture.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the wall's very thin profile, with the rough stone edges visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same irregular stone pattern and coloration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness and uneven stone edges.
attribute_location: urban park boundary, historical plaza, or city sidewalk edge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone barrier or incorporated into a low retaining wall
attribute_pattern: irregular, natural stone pattern with visible mortar joints and varied stone sizes
attribute_weathering: rough and slightly weathered, with some stones showing minor discoloration and surface pitting
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with other stone or natural landscaping elements nearby
